mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary policy that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by >0.5% in 1 month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that support corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in >50% of instances with PAYEMS growth >100k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing activity that boosts corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in >50% of instances with INDPRO growth >1.5% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) signals strong demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in >50% of instances with HOUST growth >5% quarter-over-quarter, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and interest rates (FEDFUNDS) signal a dual threat to corporate margins and discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 195 and FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates resilient demand that supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during monetary easing cycles.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in >50% of instances with RSAFS growth >2% and FEDFUNDS < 3.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 5.0% signals labor market tightness that may trigger inflationary pressures and central bank intervention, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE falls >0.5pp in 1 month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) reflects robust aggregate demand that supports equity earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in >50% of instances with GDPC1 growth >2% quarter-over-quarter, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
